frames.name: AI Assistant Evolution
frames.description: Presents the story as a significant advancement in AI assistant capabilities, focusing on Gemini's new 'agentic' features that can perform tasks autonomously
frames.emphasis: The breakthrough nature of AI assistants being able to complete real-world tasks like ordering groceries and booking rides on behalf of users

frames.name: Competitive Technology Race
frames.description: Frames the update in the context of competition between tech giants, particularly highlighting Google's advantage over Apple
frames.emphasis: Google and Samsung delivering features 'ahead of the similar features for Siri that have been delayed for months now'

frames.name: Comprehensive Feature Update
frames.description: Presents the story as a broad software update affecting multiple device generations with various enhancements
frames.emphasis: The breadth of new features across different Pixel generations, from newest to Pixel 6, showing Google's commitment to older devices

outlierPerspectives: Accessibility focus through the 'comfort view' feature that reduces visual stimulation
Cross-device ecosystem integration with desktop experience for external displays
missingContext: Privacy implications of AI assistants performing autonomous tasks with access to personal apps and data
Security considerations for AI agents acting on behalf of users in financial and personal transactions
Comparison of capabilities with existing AI assistants from other manufacturers beyond the brief Siri mention
User control mechanisms and safeguards for the autonomous AI features
Rollout timeline and geographic availability details for the 'select' countries and languages mentioned
Technical requirements or limitations for the new features
Potential impact on app developers and third-party integration requirements
